text: Becky Biberstein Michael Green Frances Carr Tom Richhart Troy Cook Bobbie Jackson Kimberly Abney Sydnee Bradburn Lean ne Lee Jeff Tillman Mike Holland and Matt Norris Sixteenth Sunday in Ordinary Time July 19 2026 This weekend July 18th 19th the Church celebrates the Sixteenth Sunday in Ordinary Time Our Gospel reading continues the parables discourse with three images of the kingdom Matthew 1324 43 the weeds sown among the wheat and left to grow until the harvest the mustard seed and the yeast These parables address Gods patience with good and evil until the final judgment and about the kingdoms small beginnings and hidden growth Parish Pictorial Directory After several delays with Universal Church Directory we were finally able to complete our Church Directory special Thank You to Michelle in the Parish Office for proof checking the directory several times over the past two months Surprisingly though we were told printing could take up to a month the directories arrived in the mail just a few days after approval As a reminder every parish family that had their picture taken or submitted their own photo will receive a complimentary copy of the Parish Pictorial Directory Complimentary directories are available in the narthex sorted by family name If you did not have your photo included but would still like a directory a limited number are available for purchase Please contact the Parish Office (260)824-1380 for more information General Renovation Update Tiling in the sanctuary began this past Monday and is expected to last three more weeks During this time the majority of other work is paused so as to not interfere with the flooring Shawnee Construction will use this pause to focus on minor repairs and touchup work for our three shrines under the supervision of our architect Bill Heyer Bill and his team have been providing thorough oversight to ensure that the quality of the work received remains top tier Liturgical Arrangements The Eucharistic Dialogue With the ongoing flooring work in the Sanctuary we are unable to open the dust screen into the construction zone and extend our makeshift sanctuary As such weve shifted our liturgical arrangements from using the versus populum altar style to the ad orientem style This change greatly shrinks the footprint for our make shift sanctuary and keeps a safe amount of space between the altar and front pew for the distribution of Holy Communion Using the ad orientem style we do not have a direct line of sight on what is happening at the altar At the same time this can be seen as an opportunity to focus more deeply upon the language used in the Eucharistic dialogue also known as The Secrets This dialogue emphasizes what is truly occurring in the transition from the Liturgy of the Word to the Liturgy of the Eucharist In the Liturgy of the Word the dialogue that is occurring is between the priest and the congregation This is clear in our back and forth responses to one another Even in those moments when there is not continuous dialogue such as during the Readings the word of God itself is spoken to us In the Liturgy of the Word we prepare our hearts to be lifted up to the Word Himself This transition from preparation to action becomes clear as we transition between our two liturgies Once the altar is prepared the priest turns towards the congregation saying Pray brothers and sisters that my sacrifice and yours might be made acceptable to the Father The congregation in response acclaims May the Lord accept the sacrifice at your hands for the praise and glory of his name for our good and the good of all his holy Church What is this sacrifice at your hands of which the congregation have just spoken of It is not only the gifts of bread and wine placed upon the altar but the very gift that is each every member of the Christian faithful themselves The priest acting in the person of Christ the Head is now entrusted by the congregation to lift up the entire Body of Christ the Church to the Father It is at this moment then that the ad orientem style begins as the priest moves from facing towards the congregation to now facing with them This Amy Gruss and Gregory Patty Gruss who passed away on Tuesday July 14th and will be laid to rest on Tuesday July 21 2026 at St Aloysius Catholic Church Yoder Eternal Rest Grant unto him Lord and let Perpetual light shine upon him May his soul and the souls of all the faithful departed through the mercy of God rest in Peace Amen OSEPH ARISH INANCE EPORT Weekend of July 25th 26th Mass Cantors 5:00pm Dan Lautzenheiser 7:30am Donna Geisel 10:00am Teresa Smith Lectors 5:00pm Bruce Holland 7:30am Christie Felger 10:00am Patti Clark Johnson Extraordinary Ministers of Holy Communion 5:00pm Dan Geimer 7:30am Beth Geisel 10:00am Rick Singer Servers 5:00pm AlexAudrey Mechling Landen Lautzenheiser 7:30am CharleneAnna Lynn Jackson Felger 10:00am GabbyOliviaJacob Hoffacker Liturgical Arrangements Cont change in direction provides a visual cue emphasizing this transition occurring within the Eucharistic dialogue All together we join our voices as one in the Preface lifting our one voice now with that of the angels Holy holy holy cf Revelation 41 11 This oneness in voice raised to the heavens then continues symbolically through the one voice of the priest as we enter completely into the Eucharistic dialogue The priest speaks through his ministerial role as Christ Himself speaking on behalf of the whole body gathered as one in the congregation As reflected in the Eucharistic dialogue He is now speaking to the Father as He unites and lift up our offerings with His one sacrifice of self upon the Cross It is worth noting especially in this moment of the liturgy that we are not a passive crowd simply watching what is happening upon the altar As our Lord lifts up our sacrifices to the Father united to His one sacrifice we are actively participating as my Christ's sacrifice and yours ours is being lifted up because we are a part of that sacrifice Through His sacrifice our very lives become acceptable sacrifices to the Father In Christ we have life All of this is occurring at every Mass regardless of the altar style that is in use Each style simply helps to emphasize different elements of the liturgy In this sense the ad orientem style forces us to a greater utilization of our spiritual senses and imagination as we enter into the Eucharistic dialogue not primarily through sight but by hearing faith and prayer For truly in the liturgy we strive to see in our hearts that which cannot be seen by sight alone Faithfully in Christ Fr David See Sign up sheet in the Narthex Mission Collection Daughters of Divine Love 395300 Knights of Columbus Father Aloysius Phillips
